content.text: Magic Will Kill You, But It Works

“Rift energy channeling is not ‘magic.’ It is the scientifically documented manipulation of extradimensional particle fields through biological neural interfaces. That it looks like magic, feels like magic, and occasionally turns the caster inside out is purely coincidental. Please refer to it as ‘Applied Dimensional Resonance’ in all official documentation.”

Magic in 3025 is ambient. It leaks from dimensional rifts, saturates the environment, and slowly poisons everything it touches. Most people avoid it. Channelers use it — pulling raw rift energy through their body and shaping it into effects called Patterns.
This is not safe. This is not clean. Every time you channel, you absorb Toxicity. The more power you pull, the more it costs. The universe charges interest.

• Rift Attunement augment (Rift Tree T1) — or a mutation/condition that grants innate channeling • A focus (rift staff, channeling rod, attuned crystal) — or bare hands at increased cost (see Unfocused Casting in Advanced Casting)
Without Rift Attunement, you cannot deliberately channel. You might still be affected by rift energy, but you can't wield it.
Single-target patterns: Interface check vs target's Evasion (same as weapon attacks). Roll to hit. On hit: full damage. On miss: no damage, costs still paid.
AoE patterns: Auto-hit the targeted tile/zone. Targets in the zone make a Reflex save (DC = 6 + caster's Interface bonus) to take half damage. No accuracy roll from the caster.
Self-buff / utility patterns: Auto-succeed. No roll needed.
Patterns are organized into four tiers based on power and cost. Higher-tier patterns require more Rift tree investment to access.
Casting at high Toxicity is dangerous. The more contaminated your body, the harder it is to shape energy cleanly. Each cast has a chance to misfire based on your current Toxicity level.

rows: 1d6 per pattern tier + prone + 1 random condition

content.text: Misfires don't fizzle. The pattern still fires at full effect — rift energy can't be contained once released. But the caster takes backlash damage (armor-ignoring) on top of all normal costs. A Torrent misfire at Toxicity 15 means: pattern fires normally, caster takes 3d6 backlash + the pattern's normal 3 HP cost. Misfires kill Channelers.
Magic isn't one skill. Different effects demand different aspects of you:

rows: Damage patterns, accuracy, complex shaping, rift manipulation
Defensive patterns, sustained effects, resisting backlash

content.label: The Channeler's Core Loop
items: Cast patterns → gain Toxicity → higher Toxicity = higher misfire risk → keep casting anyway because the enemy isn't dead yet → pray you don't roll a misfire on the big one → if you're still alive, you won
